title: How to make non-rooting containers with Podman more efficient?
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

Hi,

I have a question related to the performance of the rootless containers. 
I try to launched web applications and I run containers as systemd service with podman.
And there was a performance problem. When I run container as rootless user I have to wait a few seconds for the page to load. The application uses the postgres database too. 
If I run the containers as root, the application runs much faster. 
There is some way to improve the performance of the rootless containers?

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.2.3
API Version:  3.2.3
Go Version:   go1.15.14
Built:        Thu Sep 23 23:22:19 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body:
Please provide a `podman info` from the rootless user in question - we need to see any relevant storage options that may be set.

In a future release of RHEL, native kernel OverlayFS will be possible with rootless containers, which should have a significantly higher performance than fuse-overlayfs that is currently in use. In the meantime, you can place any files you are serving on volumes, which avoid the penalties of the FUSE filesystem.

@mheon podman info from rootless user

OK. You're on fuse-overlayfs, as expected.
@giuseppe Any speed optimizations, beyond waiting for kernel overlay to be ready?

Could it be slirp4netns?

Can you try running with `--net host`?  Do you see any improvements?
@giuseppe yes i already use it  but the page still loads for about 5 seconds
@piit12 Can you share mote details about your containers? How do you run them?

@Luap99 Actually I run two containers as systemd services.
```
[Unit]
After=redis.service postgresql.service awx-web.service
Requires=redis.service postgresql.service awx-web.service

[Service]
User=awx
Group=awx
RuntimeDirectory=%p
Restart=on-failure
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --log-driver=journald --detach --security-opt label=disable --net host --conmon-pidfile /%t/%p/pid  --cidfile /%t/%p/cid --hostname awx --user root  --volume /etc/awx/environment.sh:/etc/tower/conf.d/environment.sh  --volume /etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/pem/tls-ca-bundle.pem:/etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/pem/tls-ca-bundle.pem:ro awx:17.1.0 /usr/bin/launch_awx_task.sh
ExecStop=/usr/bin/sh -c '/usr/bin/podman rm -f $(cat /%t/%p/cid)'
PIDFile=/%t/%p/pid
KillMode=none
Type=forking
Slice=system-%p.slice
LimitNOFILE=65536

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

```
```
[Unit]
After=redis.service postgresql.service
Requires=redis.service postgresql.service

[Service]
User=awx
Group=awx
RuntimeDirectory=%p
Restart=on-failure
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --log-driver=journald --detach --security-opt label=disable --net host --conmon-pidfile /%t/%p/pid --cidfile /%t/%p/cid --hostname awxweb --user root  --volume /et/awx/environment.sh:/etc/tower/conf.d/environment.sh  --volume /etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/pem/tls-ca-bundle.pem:/etc/pki/ca-trust/extracted/pem/tls-ca-bundle.pem:ro --volume /etv/awx/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro awx:17.1.0
ExecStop=/usr/bin/sh -c '/usr/bin/podman rm -f $(cat /%t/%p/cid)'
PIDFile=/%t/%p/pid
KillMode=none
Type=forking
Slice=system-%p.slice

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target

```

@mheon when can we expect a kernel overlay?

Does it make sense to run rootless containers with `--priviliged`?
Rootless overlay is in the 5.13 kernel.  It also has been back ported to RHEL8.5 kernel.

> Does it make sense to run rootless containers with `--priviliged`?

Does it solve your issue?

Yes, it makes sense.  It tells Podman not to restrict capabilities and disable other few security checks.  The user namespace still limits the container, so it is not so insecure as running a privileged root container.

@giuseppe Unfortunately not. I did not notice any improvment.

I see, I just was thinking about security issue in this case.
@piit12 Do you have the actual db files mounted as volume? This should speed things up because it would use the host fs instead of fuse-overlay.
@Luap99 At the moment the application connects through the host and port to the database whose service is running on the same host where these 2 containers are running.
@rhatdan if i update kernel on the host do i need to install something for it to work?
You probably want to remove all of your containers.
Since we answered the question, closing.  Carry on the conversation within this issue.
